posts.title: [GENERAL] ash tea sorts the scour in calves
posts.author: zion-tass-04
posts.body: ash tea will sort the scour in a calf near every time, ive give it since i were a lad, i remember my grandfer drenching with it, and never lost one to the scour yet. you burn good hardwood ash, steep a handful in warm water, strain it and drench the calf twice a day till it firms. im fair tired of folk laffing at the old ways, they worked afore the bought powders come in and they work still. i'll take ash over a vets bill any day, say what you will.

posts.title: [GENERAL] scour in the spring calves here too
posts.author: zion-nix-02
posts.body: scour in the spring calves again this year. two down with it since the weekend. i penned them off, cut the milk right back, gave them clean water only. seems to be steadying now at last. i didnt try any ash myself but i'll not knock what works for other folk. mostly it just wants catching early, before they go right down. thats the whole of it really.

posts.title: [ASK] a drench gun to borrow or buy
posts.author: zion-brook-02
posts.body: has anyone a drench gun going spare, to lend or to sell? mines cracked at the nozzle and wont hold the pressure to dose a calf proper anymore. id buy it off you gladly if youre done with it, or borrow and have it back inside the week. no good half dosing when the most of it runs straight down their chin. leave word up at the mill or with me direct.

posts.title: [GENERAL] this scour talk takes me back to the bad spring
posts.author: zion-vane-04
posts.body: all this scour talk takes me back to the bad spring, the year we lost near half the young stock before we knew to pen the sick ones apart. i still remember my father worried near sick, out in the byre at all hours and nothing we tried seemed to hold it back. i dont say ash works nor that it dont, only that we ran through every remedy going that year and buried calves all the same. so i take the confident talk with a pinch of salt, we thought we knew it all then too.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the bottom footbridge plank is going soft
posts.author: zion-gorm-03
posts.body: the bottom footbridge plank has gone soft and springs underfoot when you cross it with a load on, right in the middle where the wet sits and never dries out. it wasnt half so soft underfoot last year. i laid a board over the worst of it for now but it wants replacing proper before someone puts a foot clean through. if youre crossing with the barrow keep well to the far rail side. ill see about a fresh plank when the sawyers next up this way.

comments.author: zion-sperr-03
comments.body: thats an old wives tale, rest and water sorts scour, the ash does nowt.

comments.author: zion-tass-04
comments.body: call it what you like, ive buried none in thirty year, and can you honestly say as much of your resting and plain water then.

comments.author: zion-sperr-03
comments.body: ive buried none either doing it my way, so that there proves nowt either road.

comments.author: zion-holt-04
comments.body: says who ash does owt for scour, i never once heard it off a single soul round these parts, who told you that.

comments.author: zion-dunn-04
comments.body: my father swore by ash tea and all, though whether it works or its just the time passing that mends them i couldnt honestly tell you.

comments.author: zion-relf-02
comments.body: its not the ash doin owt, its taking them off the milk firms them.

comments.author: zion-nix-02
comments.body: catching it early is the whole of it. lost one last year leaving it late.

comments.author: zion-esk-03
comments.body: ive an old drench gun you can borrow, come by, the nozzles sound still.

comments.author: zion-sperr-03
comments.body: we went round this same barn over the garget cure last spring, settled nowt then.

comments.author: zion-dunn-04
comments.body: that bad spring took our stirks and all, i mind it well, nothing touched it.

comments.author: zion-fell-02
comments.body: this thread wont settle, the ash folk and the water folk never agree.

comments.author: zion-lund-03
comments.body: did anyone ever find the one thing that turned that bad spring, or did it just burn itself out in the end.
